summary: First-iteration end-to-end verification of DigiConnect (FastAPI + React 19). Backend pytest suite (17 tests) and frontend Playwright checks (19 checks across two runs) all pass. Covered: health, JWT auth (login success/wrong pw/unknown email/me with-and-without token), Products CRUD with auth guard + active_only filter + create->read->update->delete persistence, Announcements CRUD with same guarantees, Mongo admin seed (bcrypt $2b$) and unique email index, all public pages (Home / Stores / About / Contact), Home sections (hero, marquee, features, products, locations, cta, footer, WhatsApp FAB), floating WhatsApp popover with two wa.me links, protected /admin route redirect, admin login (valid + invalid), admin dashboard product+announcement create/delete, admin logout, mobile hamburger below md breakpoint, SEO (title, meta description, JSON-LD dual store schema).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses deprecated FastAPI @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown'). Migrate to the lifespan context manager to silence DeprecationWarning and stay compatible with future FastAPI releases.

CORS middleware is registered AFTER app.include_router(api_router) (server.py line 325-333). This still works because middleware is applied at request time, but the conventional order is: add middleware BEFORE mounting routers to make configuration order explicit.

CORS_ORIGINS='*' combined with allow_credentials=True is not permitted by the CORS spec (browsers reject credentialed requests when origin is '*'). Currently harmless because the frontend uses Bearer tokens in headers rather than cookies, but if httpOnly cookies are later introduced, this must be replaced with an explicit origin list.

AdminDashboard.jsx is 531 lines — consider splitting ProductsPanel, AnnouncementsPanel, ProductForm, AnnouncementForm into separate files (>700 line guidance but already large enough to benefit from modularization).
Product/Announcement delete uses window.confirm dialogs (native prompt) instead of a shadcn AlertDialog — a11y/design consistency improvement, not a blocker.
Backend startup seeds admin idempotently and re-hashes password on change (matches auth_testing.md requirement) — LGTM.

Auth playbook items NOT implemented (only expected if the app opted into them): httpOnly cookies, brute-force lockout after N failures, refresh-token endpoint. Current impl is a stateless Bearer-JWT flow only, which matches the PRD ('JWT email+password').
test_credentials: admin@digiconnect.net.in / DigiConnect@2026 (verified working). Token key in localStorage: dc_admin_token.
seed_data_creation: None persisted. All TEST_-prefixed products/announcements created during tests were deleted in the same run. Backend already seeds 4 products and 4 announcements on empty collections.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Regression suite is at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — run with: `pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. It's self-contained (uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env fallback) and cleans up all TEST_-prefixed data. Frontend data-testids exercised: hero-section, announcement-marquee, site-header, products-section, locations-section, site-footer, whatsapp-fab, whatsapp-popover, whatsapp-choose-{gaur-city,grand-venice}, whatsapp-close, nav-link-{home,stores,about,contact}, mobile-menu-toggle, mobile-menu, mobile-nav-*, stores-list, about-{story,values,mission}, contact-{channels,instagram-btn}, admin-login-{form,email,password,submit,error}, tab-{products,announcements}, add-{product,announcement}-btn, product-form-{name,category,price,highlight,image,order,active}, ann-form-{message,order,active}, product-form-save (button:has-text(Save) fallback), admin-product-{id}, admin-ann-{id}, delete-product-{id}, delete-ann-{id}, admin-logout. Note: an initial screenshot captured 'No products yet' during the API-loading flash — the dashboard is fine; add an explicit await for `[data-testid^="admin-product-"]` before asserting counts.
rca of the issue: No bugs reproduced. Only observation: right after /admin login the products/announcements panels briefly render the empty state until the GET /api/products and GET /api/announcements calls resolve (~200-500ms). This is expected behavior with an empty initial useState([]) — main agent could optionally show a skeleton/spinner while loading to avoid the flash.
